title: Global Superstore Sales Dashboard
description:
As our world has been steadily shifting to digital, an unpredictable global pandemic has had a massive impact on the extent to which the shift has taken place. From the beginning of lockdowns in March 2020, nearly every aspect of our lives has changed within our daily lives such as the way we shop.
With perks like saving time, increased safety, ease of use and reduced costs for traveling by car or public transport, e-shopping has proven to be an attractive option for consumers aside from just times of necessity during the pandemic. Due to these numerous benefits more people prefer buying things online over the conventional method of going into stores.
Data allows organizations to visualize relationships between what is happening in different locations, departments, and systems. Looking at these data points side by side allows us to develop more accurate theories, and put into place more effective solutions. The goal of this project is to create a dashboard that provides actionable insights on sales by country, segment, category and subcategory.

Due to these numerous benefits more people prefer buying things online over the conventional method of going into stores. Data allows organizations to visualize relationships between what is happening in different locations, departments, and systems. Looking at these data points side by side allows us to develop more accurate theories, and put into place more effective solutions. The goal of this project is to create a dashboard that provides actionable insights on sales by country, segment, category and subcategory.

allStepDetails.content:
Global Superstore Sales Dashboard
As our world has been steadily shifting to digital, an unpredictable global pandemic has had a massive impact on the extent to which the shift has taken place. From the beginning of lockdowns in March 2020, nearly every aspect of our lives has changed within our daily lives such as the way we shop. With perks like saving time, increased safety, ease of use and reduced costs for traveling by car or public transport, e-shopping has proven to be an attractive option for consumers aside from just times of necessity during the pandemic. Due to these numerous benefits more people prefer buying things online over the conventional method of going into stores. Data allows organizations to visualize relationships between what is happening in different locations, departments, and systems. Looking at these data points side by side allows us to develop more accurate theories, and put into place more effective solutions. The goal of this project is to create a dashboard that provides actionable insights on sales by country, segment, category and subcategory.
Step Name:Data source and Data model
Detailed Instructions:

Start building our dashboard-

Gif-

1. Get the excel file by clicking on the red icon then download and save it on your computer .

 2.  Open power bi desktop and load the source file to power bi, that is  get data from website.

 3.  Start cleaning the table, like removing blank rows and duplicates.

4. Create relationship between tables. Before that, lets create Calendar table and the calculation is attached below.

5. Click on the model section of the power BI and create a relationship between calendar table and the fact table GlobalSS. 

Calendar table creation:

Getting data from excel word book

(https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/power-bi/connect-data/service-excel-workbook-files)

Global Superstore Sales Dashboard
As our world has been steadily shifting to digital, an unpredictable global pandemic has had a massive impact on the extent to which the shift has taken place. From the beginning of lockdowns in March 2020, nearly every aspect of our lives has changed within our daily lives such as the way we shop. With perks like saving time, increased safety, ease of use and reduced costs for traveling by car or public transport, e-shopping has proven to be an attractive option for consumers aside from just times of necessity during the pandemic. Due to these numerous benefits more people prefer buying things online over the conventional method of going into stores. Data allows organizations to visualize relationships between what is happening in different locations, departments, and systems. Looking at these data points side by side allows us to develop more accurate theories, and put into place more effective solutions. The goal of this project is to create a dashboard that provides actionable insights on sales by country, segment, category and subcategory.
Step Name:Steps for creating main title and logo
Detailed Instructions:
1. Choose a background. 
2. Insert text box and write the main title
3.Insert Colaberry and Global Super store logo

Global Superstore Sales Dashboard
As our world has been steadily shifting to digital, an unpredictable global pandemic has had a massive impact on the extent to which the shift has taken place. From the beginning of lockdowns in March 2020, nearly every aspect of our lives has changed within our daily lives such as the way we shop. With perks like saving time, increased safety, ease of use and reduced costs for traveling by car or public transport, e-shopping has proven to be an attractive option for consumers aside from just times of necessity during the pandemic. Due to these numerous benefits more people prefer buying things online over the conventional method of going into stores. Data allows organizations to visualize relationships between what is happening in different locations, departments, and systems. Looking at these data points side by side allows us to develop more accurate theories, and put into place more effective solutions. The goal of this project is to create a dashboard that provides actionable insights on sales by country, segment, category and subcategory.
Step Name:Line and clustered column Visual 1
Detailed Instructions:

Visual 1: Line and clustered column Visual - SalesPer Mth, Sales YoY, GPM,Discount

         Step1

Fields Tab

·         Shared Axis: Month/Year/Quarter

·         Column Values: Sales Per Mth

·         Line Values: Sales YoY,GPM,Discount

Format Tab

·    General: X Position = 177, Y Position = 65, Width = 560, Height 333

 Step2

·         Legend: On, Position = Top, Title = off, Color = #605E5C, font family = Segoe UI, Text size = 10, Style = Markers Only, Match line color = on, Circle default icon = on

·         X Axis: Color =#605E5C , Text Size = 9, Font family = Segoe UI, Minimum category width = 20, Maximum Size = 25%, Inner Padding = 20%, Concatenate labels = On, Title = off

·         Y Axis: Position = Left, Scale Type = Linear, start = Auto, End = Auto, Invert axis= off, Color = #605E5C, Text size = 10, Font family = Segoe UI, Display Units = Auto, Value decimal Places= Auto, Title = off, Gridlines = on, Line color = #CCCCC, Strock width = 2, Line Style = Dotted, show secondary = On, Align zeros = on

·         Zoom Slider = off,

·         Data Colors, Sales YoY = #A9A24E, GPM = #7AACB0, Discount=#8FA674,Default Color = #605E5C, Show all = off

·         Shapes: Shade area = On, Stroke width = 2, Join Type = Round, Line Style = Solid, show marker = on, Marker shape =., Meaker Size = 5, Marker color = #FFFFF, stepped = off, customize series = on, %MoM, Shade area = on, Stroke width = 2, Join type = round, Line style = Solid, show marker = on, Marker size = 5, Marker color = #ECC846, Stepped = off

·         Plot area Transparency = 0%

Insight: 

How to create line and clustered column chart in power bi

https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=2d1JhcrhhGo

Step Name:In this step we are going to look at how to build date slicer
Detailed Instructions:

1. Drag and drop slicer from visualization and make it in between slicer.

2. Drag and drop date from calendar table

3. Add a title text and

    Background color- White,10% darker
    Alignment - middle
   Text size -16pt
    Font family - Calibri

4. Slicer header - off

5.  Boarder - on

Color - white 30% darker

6.Length-125,Width -158

Insight: 
How to add date slicer to your dashboard ?
https://www.enhansoft.com/how-to-create-a-power-bi-date-range-slicer/

Step Name:Filled Map
Detailed Instructions:

Further, in this step we are going to create World map in order to show Sales per Mth per country

Steps for this report

1. Drag and drop Filled map from visualizations.

2. Drag and drop

 Country column from GlobalSuperStore table to location in fields box
 Sales Per Mth  field to Tooltip

3.For legend  format data colors for sales per month field ,

Minimum - Yellow
Medium  -   Pink
Maximum - Green

4. Map Style - GrayScale

Map - World

5. Zoom

Selection zoom - on

7. Add title and use same format through out the dashboard (Refer step 4)

8. Boarder - on

Color - white 30% darker

9. Tooltips - on

Insight: 

How to create a filled map in Power BI?

https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/power-bi/visuals/power-bi-visualization-filled-maps-choropleths

Step Name:Last refreshed Date and Time
Detailed Instructions:
Steps to follow-
1.Go to Data Transformation
2.Advanced Editor
3.Enter the source code
4.Click Done

5.Rename Query1 to Last Date Refreshed 

6.New column will be created with date and time 

7.Create new Measure as below and drag and drop in a key card  to the Dashboard
8.Format 

5.Dashboard including last refresh date

portfolioContent.title: Global Superstore Sales Dashboard
portfolioContent.portfolioSummary: Developed a Power BI sales dashboard delivering actionable insights across countries, segments, and categories for enhanced retail decision-making.
portfolioContent.summary: This project involved creating a comprehensive Power BI dashboard to analyze global superstore sales data by country, segment, category, and subcategory. The dashboard enables visualization of sales trends and relationships across locations and departments, facilitating data-driven strategies. It addresses shifts in consumer shopping behavior accelerated by the pandemic.
portfolioContent.businessProblem: The COVID-19 pandemic drastically changed shopping behaviors, increasing online purchases and impacting retail sales patterns globally. Retailers needed a way to understand these changes and optimize sales strategies across multiple dimensions. Without clear insights, businesses risked missing opportunities in evolving market segments and regions.
objectives: Build an interactive dashboard to visualize sales data by country, segment, category, and subcategory
Enable identification of sales trends and patterns influenced by pandemic-driven consumer behavior shifts
Provide actionable insights to support strategic retail decision-making
workflow: Gathered and prepared global superstore sales data
Built data model integrating multiple sales dimensions
Designed and developed interactive Power BI dashboard visuals
Implemented filters and slicers for dynamic data exploration
Validated insights and refined dashboard for business usability
keyInsights: Sales patterns varied significantly by country during the pandemic period
Certain product categories and subcategories showed increased online demand
Customer segments exhibited distinct purchasing behaviors influenced by lockdowns
Visualizing sales data side-by-side enabled identification of cross-departmental trends
businessImpact: Improved ability to track and respond to shifting sales trends globally
Enhanced strategic planning through data-driven insights on customer segments
Supported retail teams in optimizing product focus and resource allocation
